analyst_observations: The skill's primary value is enforcing spec-first workflow: searching specs/README.md, reading success criteria, and producing spec-formatted output. Without the skill, agents skip specs entirely.

The one failed assertion (eval 0 with_skill) was borderline: the agent correctly identified a spec gap and proposed additions, which is actually the right behavior per the skill's decision tree. The assertion 'Did NOT propose new success criteria' was too strict.
Without the skill, agents consistently jump to implementation code. All 3 baseline runs included Python/TSX code. All 3 skill runs stayed at the design level.
The spec index keyword additions (a key requirement) were produced by all 3 skill runs and zero baseline runs.
The writing-plans chaining worked perfectly: all 3 skill runs terminated with explicit writing-plans invocation. No baseline run mentioned any skill chain.
